---
title: "Reproducing published results with cier"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Reproducing published results with cier}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---



A detection index you cannot tie back to the literature is a number, not
evidence. This vignette reproduces, index by index, the results of four
published careless-responding studies whose data are public:

- **Brühlmann, Petralito, Aeschbach & Opwis (2020)** — flagged counts at the
  authors' cutoffs on the crowdsourced sample that ships with cier as
  `bfi_careless`;
- **Goldammer, Stöckli, Escher, Annen, Jonas & Antonakis (2024)** — the
  14-index detection-accuracy comparison (here called the *battery paper*),
  Studies 1-5 with experimentally induced careless responding;
- **Goldammer, Stöckli, Annen & Schmitz-Wilhelmy (2024)** — conventional vs.
  resampled personal reliability (the *RPR paper*), real-data example;
- **Schroeders, Schmidt & Gnambs (2022)** — gradient boosting vs. traditional
  indices on instructed careless responding (HEXACO-60).

For every index a paper used, the tables below show the statistic the paper
reports, the value cier reproduces, and an honest verdict: **matches** means
agreement at the paper's two-decimal precision (flagged counts: exact),
**close** means within 0.03 (counts: within 2) without two-decimal agreement,
and **differs** means beyond that, always with a written explanation. Where
cier has no corresponding functionality the cier cell is left **empty** — the
row still appears, so the table can be filled in if the package grows.

**How this document is built.** The external datasets sit on OSF and an ETH
polybox share with no redistribution licence, so cier never bundles them, and
no data is fetched when the package builds: this vignette was knitted by the
package authors from its source (`published-results.Rmd.orig`), which fetches
the data into a local cache and runs every computation shown here. The shipped
copy carries that run's output verbatim, and the assembled table is written to
`inst/extdata/published-results.csv`, where the package's test suite freezes
both the transcribed paper values and the reproduced numbers.


``` r
library(cier)
seed_resample <- 1L       # resampled indices (RPR and relatives)
seed_draws    <- 20260610L  # the Schroeders 1,000-test-draw loop
cache <- file.path(tools::R_user_dir("cier", "cache"), "published-results")
dir.create(cache, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
```

A handful of small helpers keep the per-study code below readable. The rank
AUC and the empirical SEN95 (sensitivity at 95% specificity) take scores
already oriented so that higher means more careless; `score_pairs()` scores
respondents on a fixed synonym/antonym pair list (needed where pair discovery
and scoring use different row sets); `domain_pooled()` is the battery paper's
"global = average of the scale-specific values" construction for the person-fit
indices.


``` r
fetch_cached <- function(url, file) {
  dest <- file.path(cache, file)
  if (!file.exists(dest)) {
    old <- options(timeout = 600); on.exit(options(old))
    utils::download.file(url, dest, mode = "wb", quiet = TRUE)
  }
  dest
}
auc <- function(score, label) {
  ok <- is.finite(score) & !is.na(label)
  s <- score[ok]; l <- as.logical(label[ok])
  pos <- s[l]; neg <- s[!l]
  if (!length(pos) || !length(neg)) return(NA_real_)
  r <- rank(c(pos, neg))
  (sum(r[seq_along(pos)]) - length(pos) * (length(pos) + 1) / 2) /
    (length(pos) * length(neg))
}
sen95 <- function(score, label) {
  ok <- is.finite(score) & !is.na(label)
  s <- score[ok]; l <- as.logical(label[ok])
  if (!sum(l) || !sum(!l)) return(NA_real_)
  mean(s[l] > stats::quantile(s[!l], 0.95, type = 7))
}
orient <- function(v, dir) if (dir == "lower") -as.numeric(v) else as.numeric(v)
score_pairs <- function(m, pairs) {
  if (!nrow(pairs)) return(rep(NA_real_, nrow(m)))
  a <- m[, pairs$item_i, drop = FALSE]; b <- m[, pairs$item_j, drop = FALSE]
  vapply(seq_len(nrow(m)), function(i) {
    x <- a[i, ]; y <- b[i, ]
    ok <- is.finite(x) & is.finite(y)
    if (sum(ok) < 3 || stats::sd(x[ok]) == 0 || stats::sd(y[ok]) == 0) {
      return(NA_real_)
    }
    stats::cor(x[ok], y[ok])
  }, numeric(1))
}
domain_pooled <- function(m, items, fn) {
  dom <- substr(items$scale, 1, 1)
  out <- matrix(NA_real_, nrow(m), length(unique(dom)))
  for (k in seq_along(unique(dom))) {
    sel <- dom == unique(dom)[k]
    im <- items[sel, , drop = FALSE]; im$scale <- substr(im$scale, 1, 1)
    out[, k] <- tryCatch(
      suppressWarnings(fn(m[, sel, drop = FALSE], im)),
      cier_error = function(e) rep(NA_real_, nrow(m)))
  }
  g <- rowMeans(out, na.rm = TRUE); g[is.nan(g)] <- NA_real_
  g
}
```

## The transcribed paper values

Every number a table below compares against is transcribed here, in one place,
from its primary source. The battery paper's AUCs come from supplementary
Tables S5/S6 (Study 1), S14/S15 (Study 2) and S42 (Studies 4 and 5) — the
supplement document ships inside the authors' own data archive, so the
transcription was verified against the file distributed with the data. The RPR
paper's values are its Table 9, Schroeders et al.'s its Table 3, and
Brühlmann et al.'s its Table 2 plus the Section 4 prose. Rows whose method
cier does not implement carry that as their verdict.


``` r
row0 <- function(paper, study, design, level, index, stat, pv, n,
                 status = "", note = "") {
  data.frame(paper = paper, study = study, design = design, level = level,
             index = index, paper_statistic = stat, paper_value = pv,
             cier_value = NA_real_, delta = NA_real_, status = status,
             n = n, note = note, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
}
trows <- list()
put <- function(...) trows[[length(trows) + 1L]] <<- row0(...)

# ---- battery paper: AUC per index x study x design x careless level --------
battery_auc <- list(
  "1|BS|partial" = c(RPR = .86, MD = .84, lz = .81, Synonyms = .81,
                     Gnormed = .81, Outfit = .80, Infit = .79, r_pbis = .78,
                     Ht = .77, INDCHI = .72, Antonyms = .71, Time = .68,
                     Longstring = .54, IRV = .43),
  "1|BS|full"    = c(RPR = .88, Time = .87, r_pbis = .85, Synonyms = .83,
                     Antonyms = .81, Ht = .79, MD = .79, Gnormed = .71,
                     lz = .71, INDCHI = .67, Outfit = .66, Infit = .65,
                     Longstring = .64, IRV = .62),
  "2|BS|partial" = c(lz = .81, Gnormed = .78, MD = .77, RPR = .77,
                     Outfit = .76, Synonyms = .76, r_pbis = .76, Ht = .76,
                     Infit = .75, INDCHI = .74, Antonyms = .73, Time = .52,
                     Longstring = .45, IRV = .40),
  "2|BS|full"    = c(r_pbis = .86, Time = .85, RPR = .84, Ht = .84,
                     Synonyms = .82, Antonyms = .82, INDCHI = .78, MD = .73,
                     lz = .70, Gnormed = .69, Outfit = .64, Infit = .64,
                     Longstring = .54, IRV = .53),
  "4|WS|partial" = c(RPR = .84, Synonyms = .85, Antonyms = .80, MD = .88,
                     r_pbis = .77, Gnormed = .78, Ht = .73, Time = .69,
                     lz = .58, Outfit = .59, Infit = .58, INDCHI = .56,
                     Longstring = .58, IRV = .48),
  "4|BS|partial" = c(RPR = .88, Synonyms = .89, Antonyms = .87, MD = .88,
                     r_pbis = .70, Gnormed = .78, Ht = .66, Time = .60,
                     lz = .79, Outfit = .77, Infit = .76, INDCHI = .56,
                     Longstring = .63, IRV = .47),
  "4|WS|full"    = c(RPR = .93, Synonyms = .94, Antonyms = .90, MD = .86,
                     r_pbis = .94, Gnormed = .83, Ht = .70, Time = .91,
                     lz = .72, Outfit = .69, Infit = .69, INDCHI = .49,
                     Longstring = .70, IRV = .60),
  "4|BS|full"    = c(RPR = .94, Synonyms = .94, Antonyms = .89, MD = .84,
                     r_pbis = .91, Gnormed = .79, Ht = .66, Time = .87,
                     lz = .81, Outfit = .77, Infit = .77, INDCHI = .48,
                     Longstring = .72, IRV = .61),
  "5|WS|partial" = c(RPR = .81, Synonyms = .82, Antonyms = .74, MD = .81,
                     r_pbis = .70, Gnormed = .72, Ht = .71, Time = .68,
                     lz = .50, Outfit = .50, Infit = .50, INDCHI = .46,
                     Longstring = .59, IRV = .52),
  "5|BS|partial" = c(RPR = .85, Synonyms = .86, Antonyms = .82, MD = .81,
                     r_pbis = .71, Gnormed = .70, Ht = .65, Time = .61,
                     lz = .72, Outfit = .69, Infit = .68, INDCHI = .48,
                     Longstring = .60, IRV = .57),
  "5|WS|full"    = c(RPR = .93, Synonyms = .90, Antonyms = .90, MD = .88,
                     r_pbis = .91, Gnormed = .84, Ht = .73, Time = .92,
                     lz = .71, Outfit = .67, Infit = .66, INDCHI = .64,
                     Longstring = .69, IRV = .66),
  "5|BS|full"    = c(RPR = .93, Synonyms = .90, Antonyms = .90, MD = .85,
                     r_pbis = .87, Gnormed = .80, Ht = .69, Time = .88,
                     lz = .83, Outfit = .78, Infit = .77, INDCHI = .64,
                     Longstring = .68, IRV = .67))
battery_n <- c("1|BS|partial" = 239, "1|BS|full" = 237, "2|BS|partial" = 226,
               "2|BS|full" = 227, "4|WS|partial" = 236, "4|BS|partial" = 359,
               "4|WS|full" = 244, "4|BS|full" = 363, "5|WS|partial" = 240,
               "5|BS|partial" = 360, "5|WS|full" = 242, "5|BS|full" = 361)
battery_ni <- c(
  Time   = "timing indices are outside cier's scope",
  lz     = "parametric IRT person-fit (lz) is not implemented in cier",
  Infit  = "parametric IRT person-fit (infit MSE) is not implemented in cier",
  Outfit = "parametric IRT person-fit (outfit MSE) is not implemented in cier",
  INDCHI = "model-based misfit index (INDCHI) is not implemented in cier")
companion_note <- paste("pairs derived from the careful conditions",
                        "(label-using, the paper's construction), scored on",
                        "all respondents")
for (key in names(battery_auc)) {
  k <- strsplit(key, "|", fixed = TRUE)[[1]]
  for (ix in names(battery_auc[[key]])) {
    ni <- ix %in% names(battery_ni)
    put("goldammer_battery_2024", k[1], k[2], k[3], ix, "AUC",
        battery_auc[[key]][[ix]], battery_n[[key]],
        if (ni) "not_implemented" else "",
        if (ni) unname(battery_ni[ix]) else "")
  }
  if (k[1] %in% c("4", "5")) {
    for (ix in c("Synonyms", "Antonyms")) {
      put("goldammer_battery_2024", k[1], k[2], k[3],
          paste(ix, "(careful-reference)"), "AUC",
          battery_auc[[key]][[ix]], battery_n[[key]], "", companion_note)
    }
  }
}
put("goldammer_battery_2024", "3", "two-wave", "", "(all)", "AUC",
    NA_real_, NA_integer_, "not_evaluated",
    paste("manipulation failed (Goldammer et al. 2024, Study 3): within-person",
          "t1-t2 response correlations did not differ across conditions; the",
          "authors ran no detection analysis"))

# ---- RPR paper: Table 9 (real-data example) ---------------------------------
rpr_t9 <- list(
  "6 facets"  = list(AUC = c(PR = .650, RPR25 = .687, RPR50 = .693,
                             RPR100 = .684),
                     SEN95 = c(PR = NA, RPR25 = .092, RPR50 = .113,
                               RPR100 = .105)),
  "16 facets" = list(AUC = c(PR = .933, RPR25 = .955, RPR50 = .956,
                             RPR100 = .959),
                     SEN95 = c(PR = .744, RPR25 = .815, RPR50 = .786,
                               RPR100 = .828)),
  "23 facets" = list(AUC = c(PR = .942, RPR25 = .963, RPR50 = .965,
                             RPR100 = .964),
                     SEN95 = c(PR = .749, RPR25 = .845, RPR50 = .887,
                               RPR100 = .870)))
for (pool in names(rpr_t9)) {
  for (stat in c("AUC", "SEN95")) {
    for (ix in c("PR", "RPR25", "RPR50", "RPR100")) {
      pv <- rpr_t9[[pool]][[stat]][[ix]]
      put("goldammer_rpr_2024", "", "", pool, ix, stat, pv, 359L,
          if (is.na(pv)) "not_evaluated" else "",
          if (is.na(pv)) {
            paste("the paper reports no SEN95 for conventional PR with six",
                  "facets (a false-positive rate of 5% was not reachable;",
                  "Table 9, footnote a)")
          } else "")
    }
  }
}

# ---- Schroeders et al.: Table 3 (empirical study) ---------------------------
schroeders_t3 <- list(
  Mahalanobis  = c(accuracy = .80, sensitivity = .18, specificity = .87,
                   precision = .13, `balanced accuracy` = .53),
  Antonyms     = c(accuracy = .86, sensitivity = .11, specificity = .94,
                   precision = .17, `balanced accuracy` = .53),
  `Even-odd`   = c(accuracy = .84, sensitivity = .19, specificity = .91,
                   precision = .19, `balanced accuracy` = .55),
  Longstring   = c(accuracy = .82, sensitivity = .18, specificity = .90,
                   precision = .16, `balanced accuracy` = .54),
  IRV          = c(accuracy = .83, sensitivity = .14, specificity = .90,
                   precision = .14, `balanced accuracy` = .52),
  Zh           = c(accuracy = .67, sensitivity = .38, specificity = .70,
                   precision = .12, `balanced accuracy` = .54),
  `GBM (responses)` = c(accuracy = .59, sensitivity = .61, specificity = .58,
                        precision = .14, `balanced accuracy` = .60),
  `GBM (response times)` = c(accuracy = .70, sensitivity = .56,
                             specificity = .71, precision = .18,
                             `balanced accuracy` = .64),
  `GBM (responses + times)` = c(accuracy = .70, sensitivity = .60,
                                specificity = .71, precision = .19,
                                `balanced accuracy` = .66))
schroeders_ni <- c(
  Zh = "parametric IRT person-fit (mirt Zh) is not implemented in cier",
  `GBM (responses)` = "machine-learning classifiers are outside cier's scope",
  `GBM (response times)` =
    "machine-learning classifiers are outside cier's scope",
  `GBM (responses + times)` =
    "machine-learning classifiers are outside cier's scope")
for (ix in names(schroeders_t3)) {
  for (stat in names(schroeders_t3[[ix]])) {
    ni <- ix %in% names(schroeders_ni)
    put("schroeders_2022", "", "", "", ix, stat, schroeders_t3[[ix]][[stat]],
        180L, if (ni) "not_implemented" else "",
        if (ni) unname(schroeders_ni[ix]) else "")
  }
}

# ---- Bruhlmann et al.: Table 2 + Section 4 ----------------------------------
bruhlmann_t2 <- list(
  list("Self-report aggregate", 106, "not_implemented",
       "self-report responding-tendency scales are outside cier's scope"),
  list("Bogus item", 92, "not_implemented",
       paste("direct attention-check items are outside cier's scope; the",
             "column ships in bfi_careless")),
  list("Instructed response item", 96, "not_implemented",
       paste("direct attention-check items are outside cier's scope; the",
             "column ships in bfi_careless")),
  list("Response time", 0, "not_implemented",
       paste("timing indices are outside cier's scope; the paper flagged no",
             "respondent on time")),
  list("Longstring", 25, "", ""),
  list("Odd-even consistency", 63, "", ""),
  list("Resampled individual reliability", 63, "", ""),
  list("Person-total correlation", 74, "", ""),
  list("Open-answer quality", 100, "not_implemented",
       "open-answer quality coding is outside cier's scope"),
  list("Latent profile class (careless)", 181, "not_implemented",
       paste("model-based classification (latent profile analysis) is",
             "outside cier's scope")))
for (x in bruhlmann_t2) {
  put("bruhlmann_2020", "", "", "", x[[1]], "n flagged", x[[2]], 394L,
      x[[3]], x[[4]])
}
paper_tab <- do.call(rbind, trows)
nrow(paper_tab)
#> [1] 264
```

## Brühlmann et al. (2020): flagged counts at the authors' cutoffs

The four statistical indicators in the paper were all computed on the 44 BFI
items, which ship with cier (`bfi_careless`), so the reproduction needs no
download. The cutoffs are the authors' own (their analysis script on OSF):
longstring above 22 (half the items), and a negative or undefined value for
odd-even consistency, resampled individual reliability (100 random
split-halves), and the person-total correlation — in all three, an undefined
value (a straightliner's zero variance) counts as flagged.


``` r
bfi <- as.matrix(bfi_careless[, 1:44])
bfi_items <- data.frame(
  scale = sub("^v_BFI_([A-Za-z]+)[0-9].*$", "\\1", colnames(bfi)),
  reverse_keyed = grepl("_R$", colnames(bfi)), categories = 5L)
ls_v  <- cier_longstring(bfi)$value
oec_v <- cier_even_odd(bfi, bfi_items)$value           # value = -SB(r)
rir_v <- cier_personal_reliability(bfi, bfi_items, n_resamples = 100L,
                                   seed = seed_resample)$value
ptc_v <- cier_person_total(bfi)$value
bruhlmann_res <- data.frame(
  paper = "bruhlmann_2020", study = "", design = "", level = "",
  paper_statistic = "n flagged",
  index = c("Longstring", "Odd-even consistency",
            "Resampled individual reliability", "Person-total correlation"),
  cier_value = c(sum(ls_v > 22),
                 sum(oec_v > 0 | is.na(oec_v)),   # -SB > 0  <=>  SB < 0
                 sum(rir_v > 0 | is.na(rir_v)),
                 sum(ptc_v < 0 | is.na(ptc_v))),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
bruhlmann_res[, c("index", "cier_value")]
#>                              index cier_value
#> 1                       Longstring         25
#> 2             Odd-even consistency         89
#> 3 Resampled individual reliability         86
#> 4         Person-total correlation         73
```

The two direct checks the paper pairs these with are also in the bundled data
and reproduce exactly with two lines of base R:


``` r
sum(bfi_careless$v_Bogus_Item >= 3)   # paper: 92 failed the bogus item
#> [1] 92
sum(bfi_careless$v_IRI != 0)          # paper: 96 failed the instructed item
#> [1] 96
```

One quirk in the authors' pipeline matters for the two consistency counts.
Their analysis script applies an extra transformation to a single openness
item after the usual reverse-coding (`v_BFI_OP9_R <- 8 - v_BFI_OP9_R`, with
the comment "wrong item"), evidently correcting an item their survey had
keyed differently than its name suggests. cier keys the bundled items by
their names, so its odd-even and resampled-reliability counts come out higher
than the paper's. The check below replicates the authors' construction with
and without that one line: with it, the paper's count, mean and SD reproduce
exactly — the divergence is that single item's keying, not the statistic.


``` r
their_oec <- function(op9_fix) {
  df2 <- bfi
  rev <- grepl("_R$", colnames(df2))
  df2[, rev] <- 6 - df2[, rev]
  if (op9_fix) df2[, "v_BFI_OP9_R"] <- 8 - df2[, "v_BFI_OP9_R"]
  scales <- sub("^v_BFI_([A-Za-z]+)[0-9].*$", "\\1", colnames(df2))
  vapply(seq_len(nrow(df2)), function(i) {
    v1 <- v2 <- numeric(0)
    for (sc in unique(scales)) {
      cols <- which(scales == sc)
      v1 <- c(v1, mean(df2[i, cols[seq_along(cols) %% 2 == 1]]))
      v2 <- c(v2, mean(df2[i, cols[seq_along(cols) %% 2 == 0]]))
    }
    suppressWarnings(stats::cor(v1, v2))
  }, numeric(1))
}
for (fix in c(FALSE, TRUE)) {
  r <- their_oec(fix)
  cat(sprintf("op9 corrected = %-5s: flagged %d, M = %.2f, SD = %.2f\n",
              fix, sum(r < 0 | is.na(r)), mean(r, na.rm = TRUE),
              stats::sd(r, na.rm = TRUE)))
}
#> op9 corrected = FALSE: flagged 89, M = 0.51, SD = 0.50
#> op9 corrected = TRUE : flagged 63, M = 0.61, SD = 0.43
# the paper's Table 2 reports 63 flagged, M = .61, SD = .43
```

## Goldammer et al. (2024), battery paper: detection AUCs

The data archive carries Studies 1 and 2 (single wave; respondents randomly
assigned to respond carefully, partially carelessly, or fully carelessly) and
Studies 4 and 5 (two waves; everyone honest at t1, the careless instruction
applied at t2). Because the label is the random assignment, it is independent
of the responses. Study 3's manipulation failed, so the paper reports no
detection analysis for it.

Per study, every index is computed on the full (per-wave) sample: the five
matrix-only indices on the raw responses; the resampled personal reliability
on random split-halves *within each of the 15 BFI-2 facets* (reverse-keyed
items declared via the item metadata); Gnormed and Ht per Big Five domain,
reverse-keyed, then averaged — the paper's "global" construction. Synonyms
and antonyms use cier's default whole-sample pairing at the paper's
`critical_r = .60`.


``` r
battery_zip <- fetch_cached(
  "https://polybox.ethz.ch/index.php/s/88QNfcknKt9JHQm/download",
  "goldammer-battery.zip")
battery_dir <- file.path(cache, "battery", "Careless responding revisted")
if (!dir.exists(battery_dir)) {
  utils::unzip(battery_zip, exdir = file.path(cache, "battery"))
}

battery_wave <- function(d, suffix = "") {
  base <- sub(paste0(suffix, "$"), "",
              grep(sprintf("^[eacno]_[a-z]+[0-9]+%s$", suffix), names(d),
                   value = TRUE))
  m <- as.matrix(d[, paste0(base, suffix)])
  storage.mode(m) <- "double"
  colnames(m) <- base
  twin <- vapply(base, function(x) {     # the archive stores reverse-scored
    paste0(x, "R", suffix) %in% names(d) ||  # twin columns; their presence
      paste0(x, suffix, "R") %in% names(d)   # marks the reverse-keyed items
  }, logical(1))
  list(m = m, items = data.frame(scale = sub("[0-9]+$", "", base),
                                 reverse_keyed = unname(twin),
                                 categories = 6L))
}
battery_values <- function(w) {
  list(
    RPR = list(v = cier_personal_reliability(w$m, w$items,
                                             seed = seed_resample)$value,
               dir = "upper"),
    MD = list(v = cier_mahalanobis(w$m)$value, dir = "upper"),
    r_pbis = list(v = cier_person_total(w$m)$value, dir = "lower"),
    Longstring = list(v = cier_longstring(w$m)$value, dir = "upper"),
    IRV = list(v = cier_irv(w$m)$value, dir = "lower"),
    Gnormed = list(v = domain_pooled(w$m, w$items, function(m, im) {
      cier_gnormed(m, im, seed = seed_resample)$value
    }), dir = "upper"),
    Ht = list(v = domain_pooled(w$m, w$items, function(m, im) {
      cier_ht(m, im)$value
    }), dir = "lower"),
    Synonyms = list(
      v = suppressWarnings(cier_psychsyn(w$m, critical_r = 0.60)$value),
      dir = "lower"),
    Antonyms = list(
      v = suppressWarnings(cier_psychant(w$m, critical_r = 0.60)$value),
      dir = "upper"))
}
battery_rows <- list()
bput <- function(study, design, level, index, score, dir, label) {
  s <- orient(score, dir)
  battery_rows[[length(battery_rows) + 1L]] <<- data.frame(
    paper = "goldammer_battery_2024", study = study, design = design,
    level = level, index = index, paper_statistic = "AUC",
    cier_value = auc(s, label),
    nfin = sum(is.finite(score) & !is.na(label)), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
}

for (study in c("1", "2")) {
  d <- utils::read.csv(file.path(battery_dir, paste0("Study ", study),
                                 sprintf("Study_%s.csv", study)),
                       check.names = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  vals <- battery_values(battery_wave(d))
  for (lv in c("partial", "full")) {
    lab <- as.integer(d[[if (lv == "partial") "careless_33" else
      "careless_100"]])
    for (nm in names(vals)) {
      bput(study, "BS", lv, nm, vals[[nm]]$v, vals[[nm]]$dir, lab)
    }
  }
}
for (study in c("4", "5")) {
  d <- utils::read.csv(file.path(battery_dir, paste0("Study ", study),
                                 sprintf("Study_%s_wide.csv", study)),
                       check.names = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  w1 <- battery_wave(d, "_t1"); w2 <- battery_wave(d, "_t2")
  v1 <- battery_values(w1);     v2 <- battery_values(w2)
  cond <- as.integer(d$condition)   # 1, 2 careful; 3 fully; 4 partially
  # companion cells: the paper derived its synonym/antonym pairs from the
  # known-careful respondents -- reproduce that label-using construction by
  # discovering pairs on the careful conditions' t2 and scoring everyone
  careful <- w2$m[cond %in% c(1L, 2L), , drop = FALSE]
  for (anto in c(FALSE, TRUE)) {
    pr <- cier_synonym_pairs(careful, critical_r = 0.60, antonym = anto)
    nm <- paste(if (anto) "Antonyms" else "Synonyms", "(careful-reference)")
    v1[[nm]] <- list(v = score_pairs(w1$m, pr),
                     dir = if (anto) "upper" else "lower")
    v2[[nm]] <- list(v = score_pairs(w2$m, pr), dir = v1[[nm]]$dir)
  }
  for (lv in c("partial", "full")) {
    cc <- if (lv == "full") 3L else 4L
    lab_bs <- rep(NA_integer_, nrow(d))
    lab_bs[cond %in% c(1L, 2L)] <- 0L; lab_bs[cond == cc] <- 1L
    rws <- which(cond == cc)
    lab_ws <- c(rep(0L, length(rws)), rep(1L, length(rws)))
    for (nm in names(v1)) {
      bput(study, "BS", lv, nm, v2[[nm]]$v, v2[[nm]]$dir, lab_bs)
      bput(study, "WS", lv, nm, c(v1[[nm]]$v[rws], v2[[nm]]$v[rws]),
           v1[[nm]]$dir, lab_ws)
    }
  }
}
battery_res <- do.call(rbind, battery_rows)
```

**Did the paper's imputation step matter?** The battery paper pooled its
estimates over 100 multiple imputations because a few indices were
incomputable for some (mostly fully careless) respondents. cier abstains on an
undefined statistic instead of imputing it — imputing a straightliner's
missing consistency score risks inventing the very signal being measured. The
check below imputes the abstained index values with `mice` and re-scores the
cell with the most abstentions: the pooled AUC moves by less than 0.005, so
the comparisons in this vignette are not driven by the imputation step (on the
companion synonym/antonym cells, imputation in fact pulls the AUC *away* from
the paper's value, because the imputed straightliners land mid-scale).


``` r
if (requireNamespace("mice", quietly = TRUE)) {
  d4 <- utils::read.csv(file.path(battery_dir, "Study 4", "Study_4_wide.csv"),
                        check.names = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  w2 <- battery_wave(d4, "_t2")
  cond <- as.integer(d4$condition)
  lab <- rep(NA_integer_, nrow(d4))
  lab[cond %in% c(1L, 2L)] <- 0L; lab[cond == 3L] <- 1L
  vm <- data.frame(
    r_pbis = cier_person_total(w2$m)$value,
    MD = cier_mahalanobis(w2$m)$value,
    IRV = cier_irv(w2$m)$value)
  set.seed(seed_resample)
  imp <- mice::mice(vm, m = 20, maxit = 10, printFlag = FALSE)
  pooled <- mean(vapply(seq_len(20), function(k) {
    auc(-mice::complete(imp, k)$r_pbis, lab)
  }, numeric(1)))
  c(complete_case = round(auc(-vm$r_pbis, lab), 3),
    mice_pooled = round(pooled, 3), n_imputed = sum(is.na(vm$r_pbis)))
}
#> complete_case   mice_pooled     n_imputed 
#>         0.900         0.901        10.000
```

## Goldammer et al. (2024), RPR paper: Table 9

The real-data example is an experiment with Swiss Armed Forces respondents
(n = 359): a control condition answered everything carefully, and two careless
conditions answered half of the scales randomly ("anything") or oppositely
("opposite") — together the careless group (n = 238). The paper's conventional
personal reliability (PR) *is* the even-odd consistency, so it maps to
`cier_even_odd()`; the resampled versions map to
`cier_personal_reliability()` with 25, 50 and 100 resamples (25 with mean
aggregation is both the paper's recommendation and cier's default). Each is
computed on three nested facet pools of the leadership questionnaire battery.


``` r
rpr_zip <- fetch_cached(
  "https://polybox.ethz.ch/index.php/s/OUeUYS9ZHkyu7YB/download",
  "goldammer-rpr.zip")
rpr_dir <- file.path(
  cache, "rpr", "Comparing conventional and resampled personal reliability")
if (!dir.exists(rpr_dir)) utils::unzip(rpr_zip, exdir = file.path(cache, "rpr"))
rd <- utils::read.csv(file.path(rpr_dir, "real_data_example_240118.csv"),
                      check.names = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

rpr_facets <- list(
  mlq_is = paste0("mlq_is", 1:4),   mlq_iib = paste0("mlq_iib", 1:4),
  mlq_iia = paste0("mlq_iia", 1:4), mlq_ic = paste0("mlq_ic", 1:4),
  mlq_im = paste0("mlq_im", 1:4),   mlq_app = paste0("mlq_app", 1:4),
  mlq_mbp = paste0("mlq_mbp", 1:4), mlq_lf = paste0("mlq_lf", 1:4),
  alq_t = paste0("alq_t", 1:5),     alq_m = paste0("alq_m", 1:4),
  alq_pb = paste0("alq_pb", 1:3),   alq_sa = paste0("alq_sa", 1:4),
  lmx_a = paste0("lmx_a", 1:3),     lmx_l = paste0("lmx_l", 1:3),
  lmx_pr = paste0("lmx_pr", 1:3),   lmx_e = paste0("lmx_e", 1:3),
  oca = paste0("oca", 1:5),         occ = paste0("occ", 1:4),
  ocn = paste0("ocn", 1:5),
  ocb_h = paste0("ocb_h", 1:5),     ocb_g = paste0("ocb_g", 1:5),
  ocb_u = paste0("ocb_u", 1:5),     ocb_e = paste0("ocb_e", 1:5))
rpr_pools <- list(`6 facets` = names(rpr_facets)[1:6],
                  `16 facets` = names(rpr_facets)[1:16],
                  `23 facets` = names(rpr_facets))
rpr_rev <- c("oca2", paste0("ocb_u", 1:5))  # items with reverse-scored twins
rpr_lab <- as.integer(rd$carel_grp)         # 0 careful (121), 1 careless (238)

rpr_rows <- list()
for (pool in names(rpr_pools)) {
  fs <- rpr_pools[[pool]]
  its <- unlist(rpr_facets[fs], use.names = FALSE)
  m <- as.matrix(rd[, its]); storage.mode(m) <- "double"
  im <- data.frame(scale = rep(fs, lengths(rpr_facets[fs])),
                   reverse_keyed = its %in% rpr_rev, categories = 5L)
  vals <- list(PR = cier_even_odd(m, im)$value)
  for (nr in c(25L, 50L, 100L)) {
    vals[[paste0("RPR", nr)]] <- cier_personal_reliability(
      m, im, n_resamples = nr, seed = seed_resample)$value
  }
  for (nm in names(vals)) {
    rpr_rows[[length(rpr_rows) + 1L]] <- data.frame(
      paper = "goldammer_rpr_2024", study = "", design = "", level = pool,
      index = nm, AUC = auc(vals[[nm]], rpr_lab),
      SEN95 = sen95(vals[[nm]], rpr_lab), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  }
}
rpr_wide <- do.call(rbind, rpr_rows)
# pipeline check: on the six-facet pool, cier's even-odd value IS the
# authors' stored PR column, sign-flipped (cier stores -SB)
f6 <- unlist(rpr_facets[rpr_pools[["6 facets"]]], use.names = FALSE)
round(stats::cor(
  cier_even_odd(as.matrix(rd[, f6]),
                data.frame(scale = rep(rpr_pools[["6 facets"]],
                                       lengths(rpr_facets[1:6])),
                           reverse_keyed = f6 %in% rpr_rev,
                           categories = 5L))$value,
  rd$PR_F6, use = "pairwise.complete.obs"), 4)
#> [1] -1
```

## Schroeders et al. (2022): classification metrics

The empirical study assigned respondents to a regular or an
instructed-inattentive condition (the analysis label is that assignment;
n = 361 vs. 244). The paper evaluates each detector on 1,000 random test
samples of 162 regular + 18 careless respondents (a realistic 10% prevalence)
and reports the mean of five classification metrics. The flag rules below are
the authors' own (their analysis scripts on OSF): Mahalanobis distance flagged
at the 95% confidence level *within each draw*; psychometric antonyms paired
at `critical_r = .20` with an undefined score counting as not flagged and a
positive within-person correlation flagged; even-odd consistency on the six
HEXACO scales in the authors' item order, flagged when negative; longstring of
six or more identical answers on the *unrecoded* responses; and the
intra-individual response variability flagging each draw's bottom decile —
the paper computed IRV both ways ("calculated twice and the higher accuracy
rates were taken") and on this data the lower tail is the better side, as
instructed-inattentive respondents straightline: it reproduces all five
Table-3 metrics where the upper tail does not. Their exact 1,000 draws are
not recoverable, so the loop below redraws with a recorded seed — the
Monte-Carlo error of a mean over 1,000 draws is ~0.002, well inside the
two-decimal precision the paper reports.


``` r
sch_csv <- fetch_cached("https://osf.io/download/brfmz/",
                        "schroeders-data_mod_resp.csv")
sch <- utils::read.csv2(sch_csv, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
sch$X <- NULL
he <- grep("^HE01_", names(sch), value = TRUE)
recoded <- as.matrix(sch[, he]); storage.mode(recoded) <- "double"
unrecoded <- recoded                       # undo the authors' reverse-coding
rcols <- grep("_r$", colnames(unrecoded))  # to recover answers as clicked,
unrecoded[, rcols] <- 6 - unrecoded[, rcols]  # in questionnaire order
unrecoded <- unrecoded[, order(as.integer(sub("^HE01_(\\d+).*$", "\\1",
                                              colnames(unrecoded))))]
eo_items <- paste0("HE01_", c(  # the authors' scale-blocked item order
  "06", "30_r", "54", "12_r", "36", "60_r", "18", "42_r", "24_r", "48_r",
  "05", "29", "53_r", "11", "35_r", "17", "41_r", "23", "47", "59_r",
  "04", "28_r", "52_r", "10_r", "34", "58", "16", "40", "22", "46_r",
  "03", "27", "09_r", "33", "51", "15_r", "39", "57_r", "21_r", "45",
  "02", "26_r", "08", "32_r", "14_r", "38", "50", "20_r", "44_r", "56_r",
  "01_r", "25", "07", "31_r", "13", "37", "49_r", "19_r", "43", "55_r"))
eo_meta <- data.frame(scale = rep(c("hh", "em", "ex", "ag", "co", "op"),
                                  each = 10),
                      reverse_keyed = FALSE, categories = 5L)

draw_flags <- function(rows) {
  m <- recoded[rows, , drop = FALSE]
  ant <- cier_psychant(m, critical_r = 0.20)$value
  ant[is.na(ant)] <- 0
  eo <- cier_even_odd(m[, eo_items, drop = FALSE], eo_meta)$value
  irv <- cier_irv(m)$value
  list(Mahalanobis = cier_mahalanobis(m, alpha = 0.05)$flagged %in% TRUE,
       Antonyms = ant > 0,
       `Even-odd` = eo > 0 & !is.na(eo),    # -SB > 0  <=>  consistency < 0
       Longstring = cier_longstring(unrecoded[rows, , drop = FALSE])$value >= 6,
       IRV = irv <= stats::quantile(irv, 0.10, type = 7))
}
metrics <- c("accuracy", "sensitivity", "specificity", "precision",
             "balanced accuracy")
careless_rows <- which(sch$Careless == 1L)
regular_rows <- which(sch$Careless == 0L)
indices <- c("Mahalanobis", "Antonyms", "Even-odd", "Longstring", "IRV")
acc <- array(NA_real_, c(1000L, 5L, 5L),
             dimnames = list(NULL, indices, metrics))
set.seed(seed_draws)
for (i in seq_len(1000L)) {
  rows <- c(sample(regular_rows, 162L), sample(careless_rows, 18L))
  l <- sch$Careless[rows] == 1L
  fl <- draw_flags(rows)
  for (nm in names(fl)) {
    f <- fl[[nm]]
    sens <- sum(f & l) / sum(l); spec <- sum(!f & !l) / sum(!l)
    acc[i, nm, ] <- c(mean(f == l), sens, spec,
                      if (sum(f)) sum(f & l) / sum(f) else NA_real_,
                      (sens + spec) / 2)
  }
}
schroeders_res <- expand.grid(index = dimnames(acc)[[2]],
                              paper_statistic = metrics,
                              stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
schroeders_res$paper <- "schroeders_2022"
schroeders_res$study <- schroeders_res$design <- schroeders_res$level <- ""
schroeders_res$cier_value <- mapply(function(ix, st) {
  mean(acc[, ix, st], na.rm = TRUE)
}, schroeders_res$index, schroeders_res$paper_statistic)
```

## Assembling the table

The computed values join the transcribed paper values; the verdicts and the
machine-readable copy of this whole comparison are derived here and written to
`inst/extdata/published-results.csv`. Statuses follow the wording rules from
the introduction and are computed from the rounded (3-decimal) stored values.


``` r
rpr_res <- rbind(
  data.frame(rpr_wide[, c("paper", "study", "design", "level", "index")],
             paper_statistic = "AUC", cier_value = rpr_wide$AUC,
             stringsAsFactors = FALSE),
  data.frame(rpr_wide[, c("paper", "study", "design", "level", "index")],
             paper_statistic = "SEN95", cier_value = rpr_wide$SEN95,
             stringsAsFactors = FALSE))
computed <- rbind(
  data.frame(battery_res[, !(names(battery_res) == "nfin")],
             stringsAsFactors = FALSE),
  rpr_res, schroeders_res[, names(rpr_res)],
  bruhlmann_res[, names(rpr_res)])
computed$cier_value <- round(computed$cier_value, 3)

tab <- merge(paper_tab, computed,
             by = c("paper", "study", "design", "level", "index",
                    "paper_statistic"),
             all.x = TRUE, suffixes = c("", "_new"))
# only rows whose verdict is still open take a computed value; the
# not_implemented and not_evaluated rows keep their empty cier cell
tab$cier_value <- ifelse(tab$status == "", tab$cier_value_new, NA_real_)
tab$cier_value_new <- NULL
tab$delta <- ifelse(!is.na(tab$paper_value) & !is.na(tab$cier_value),
                    round(tab$cier_value - tab$paper_value, 3), NA_real_)

counts <- tab$paper == "bruhlmann_2020"
explain <- function(paper, index, design, statistic) {
  if (paper == "goldammer_battery_2024" && index == "MD") {
    return(paste("cier centres the Mahalanobis distance on the whole sample;",
                 "the authors used the careful respondents as the reference",
                 "group, which an applied screen does not know"))
  }
  if (paper == "goldammer_battery_2024" && index == "Ht") {
    return(paste("the per-domain scalability is computed on the full wave",
                 "rather than the paper's careful-referenced construction:",
                 "on the two-wave studies this separates the within-person",
                 "contrast more strongly and the between-group contrast",
                 "slightly less; the statistic itself matches mokken"))
  }
  if (paper == "goldammer_battery_2024" && index == "Synonyms") {
    return(paste("whole-sample pairing finds only a few attenuated synonym",
                 "pairs on the careless second wave (and the two waves pair",
                 "differently), depressing the score; the careful-reference",
                 "rows show the paper's label-using construction"))
  }
  if (grepl("careful-reference", index)) {
    return(paste(companion_note, "- the discovered pair set is not identical",
                 "to the authors' (their exact derivation is unpublished)"))
  }
  if (paper == "goldammer_rpr_2024" && statistic == "SEN95") {
    return(paste("the paper estimates SEN95 with tie-corrected nonparametric",
                 "ROC regression; this check uses the plain empirical",
                 "95%-specificity cutoff"))
  }
  if (index == "Odd-even consistency") {
    return(paste("the authors' script applies an extra correction to one",
                 "mis-keyed openness item; with it replicated, their count,",
                 "mean and SD reproduce exactly (demonstrated above)"))
  }
  if (index == "Resampled individual reliability") {
    return(paste("the authors' one-item keying correction (see odd-even",
                 "consistency) plus their raw-correlation averaging without",
                 "the Spearman-Brown step; with both replicated their count",
                 "reproduces to within resampling noise"))
  }
  if (index == "Person-total correlation") {
    return(paste("the authors correlate each person with the item means of",
                 "the remaining respondents (leave-one-out); cier uses the",
                 "whole-sample item means"))
  }
  ""
}
for (i in which(tab$status == "")) {
  if (is.na(tab$cier_value[i])) {
    tab$status[i] <- "abstains"
    tab$note[i] <- paste("whole-sample pairing at critical_r = .60 finds no",
                         "qualifying item pairs on the careless-contaminated",
                         "responses, so the index abstains by design")
  } else if (is.na(tab$paper_value[i])) {
    tab$status[i] <- "not_evaluated"
  } else if (!counts[i]) {
    tab$status[i] <-
      if (round(tab$cier_value[i], 2) == round(tab$paper_value[i], 2)) {
        "matches"
      } else if (abs(tab$delta[i]) <= 0.03 + 1e-9) "close" else "differs"
  } else {
    tab$status[i] <- if (tab$delta[i] == 0) {
      "matches"
    } else if (abs(tab$delta[i]) <= 2) "close" else "differs"
  }
  if (tab$status[i] == "differs" && !nzchar(tab$note[i])) {
    tab$note[i] <- explain(tab$paper[i], tab$index[i], tab$design[i],
                           tab$paper_statistic[i])
  }
}
# abstention visibility: how many respondents each battery cell scored
bk <- paste(battery_res$study, battery_res$design, battery_res$level,
            battery_res$index)
tk <- paste(tab$study, tab$design, tab$level, tab$index)
for (i in which(tab$paper == "goldammer_battery_2024" &
                  !is.na(tab$cier_value))) {
  nfin <- battery_res$nfin[match(tk[i], bk)]
  if (!is.na(nfin) && nfin < tab$n[i]) {
    tab$note[i] <- sub("^; ", "", paste0(
      tab$note[i], "; scored on ", nfin, " of ", tab$n[i],
      " respondents (abstentions excluded)"))
  }
}
tab <- tab[order(tab$paper, tab$study, tab$design, tab$level, tab$index,
                 tab$paper_statistic, method = "radix"), ]
utils::write.csv(tab, file.path("inst", "extdata", "published-results.csv"),
                 row.names = FALSE, na = "")
table(tab$status)
#> 
#>        abstains           close         differs         matches   not_evaluated 
#>              16              44              34              82               2 
#> not_implemented 
#>              86
```

## The results, paper by paper



### Brühlmann et al. (2020)


|Indicator                        | Paper: n flagged| cier|Verdict         |
|:--------------------------------|----------------:|----:|:---------------|
|Bogus item                       |               92|     |not implemented |
|Instructed response item         |               96|     |not implemented |
|Latent profile class (careless)  |              181|     |not implemented |
|Longstring                       |               25|   25|matches         |
|Odd-even consistency             |               63|   89|differs         |
|Open-answer quality              |              100|     |not implemented |
|Person-total correlation         |               74|   73|close           |
|Resampled individual reliability |               63|   86|differs         |
|Response time                    |                0|     |not implemented |
|Self-report aggregate            |              106|     |not implemented |

The deterministic indicators reproduce the paper's counts exactly. The two
that involve a resampling stream or a slightly different averaging convention
(resampled individual reliability, person-total correlation) land within a
respondent or two of the paper, for the reasons in their table notes: the flag
rule is the *sign* of a consistency score, so only respondents sitting almost
exactly at zero can move between runs.

### Goldammer et al. (2024), battery paper


**Study 1 - between-subject design (AUC)**



|Index      | Paper (partial)| cier (partial)| Paper (full)| cier (full)|Verdict (partial / full) |
|:----------|---------------:|--------------:|------------:|-----------:|:------------------------|
|RPR        |            0.86|          0.859|         0.88|       0.887|matches / close          |
|Time       |            0.68|               |         0.87|            |not implemented          |
|r_pbis     |            0.78|          0.776|         0.85|       0.852|matches                  |
|Synonyms   |            0.81|               |         0.83|            |abstains                 |
|Antonyms   |            0.71|               |         0.81|            |abstains                 |
|Ht         |            0.77|          0.766|         0.79|       0.786|matches                  |
|MD         |            0.84|          0.837|         0.79|       0.759|matches / differs        |
|Gnormed    |            0.81|          0.798|         0.71|       0.704|close                    |
|lz         |            0.81|               |         0.71|            |not implemented          |
|INDCHI     |            0.72|               |         0.67|            |not implemented          |
|Outfit     |            0.80|               |         0.66|            |not implemented          |
|Infit      |            0.79|               |         0.65|            |not implemented          |
|Longstring |            0.54|          0.548|         0.64|       0.646|close                    |
|IRV        |            0.43|          0.428|         0.62|       0.616|matches                  |


**Study 2 - between-subject design (AUC)**



|Index      | Paper (partial)| cier (partial)| Paper (full)| cier (full)|Verdict (partial / full) |
|:----------|---------------:|--------------:|------------:|-----------:|:------------------------|
|r_pbis     |            0.76|          0.759|         0.86|       0.858|matches                  |
|Time       |            0.52|               |         0.85|            |not implemented          |
|Ht         |            0.76|          0.771|         0.84|       0.840|close / matches          |
|RPR        |            0.77|          0.769|         0.84|       0.838|matches                  |
|Antonyms   |            0.73|               |         0.82|            |abstains                 |
|Synonyms   |            0.76|               |         0.82|            |abstains                 |
|INDCHI     |            0.74|               |         0.78|            |not implemented          |
|MD         |            0.77|          0.799|         0.73|       0.725|close                    |
|lz         |            0.81|               |         0.70|            |not implemented          |
|Gnormed    |            0.78|          0.777|         0.69|       0.695|matches / close          |
|Infit      |            0.75|               |         0.64|            |not implemented          |
|Outfit     |            0.76|               |         0.64|            |not implemented          |
|Longstring |            0.45|          0.438|         0.54|       0.542|close / matches          |
|IRV        |            0.40|          0.399|         0.53|       0.532|matches                  |


**Study 4 - between-subject design (AUC)**



|Index                        | Paper (partial)| cier (partial)| Paper (full)| cier (full)|Verdict (partial / full) |
|:----------------------------|---------------:|--------------:|------------:|-----------:|:------------------------|
|RPR                          |            0.88|          0.883|         0.94|       0.939|matches                  |
|Synonyms                     |            0.89|          0.734|         0.94|       0.849|differs                  |
|Synonyms (careful-reference) |            0.89|          0.888|         0.94|       0.934|matches / close          |
|r_pbis                       |            0.70|          0.697|         0.91|       0.900|matches / close          |
|Antonyms                     |            0.87|               |         0.89|            |abstains                 |
|Antonyms (careful-reference) |            0.87|          0.879|         0.89|       0.941|close / differs          |
|Time                         |            0.60|               |         0.87|            |not implemented          |
|MD                           |            0.88|          0.835|         0.84|       0.738|differs                  |
|lz                           |            0.79|               |         0.81|            |not implemented          |
|Gnormed                      |            0.78|          0.780|         0.79|       0.792|matches                  |
|Infit                        |            0.76|               |         0.77|            |not implemented          |
|Outfit                       |            0.77|               |         0.77|            |not implemented          |
|Longstring                   |            0.63|          0.634|         0.72|       0.702|matches / close          |
|Ht                           |            0.66|          0.648|         0.66|       0.641|close                    |
|IRV                          |            0.47|          0.472|         0.61|       0.608|matches                  |
|INDCHI                       |            0.56|               |         0.48|            |not implemented          |


**Study 4 - within-subject design (AUC)**



|Index                        | Paper (partial)| cier (partial)| Paper (full)| cier (full)|Verdict (partial / full) |
|:----------------------------|---------------:|--------------:|------------:|-----------:|:------------------------|
|r_pbis                       |            0.77|          0.761|         0.94|       0.930|close                    |
|Synonyms                     |            0.85|          0.583|         0.94|       0.782|differs                  |
|Synonyms (careful-reference) |            0.85|          0.836|         0.94|       0.939|close / matches          |
|RPR                          |            0.84|          0.844|         0.93|       0.937|matches / close          |
|Time                         |            0.69|               |         0.91|            |not implemented          |
|Antonyms                     |            0.80|               |         0.90|            |abstains                 |
|Antonyms (careful-reference) |            0.80|          0.820|         0.90|       0.931|close / differs          |
|MD                           |            0.88|          0.636|         0.86|       0.647|differs                  |
|Gnormed                      |            0.78|          0.781|         0.83|       0.827|matches                  |
|lz                           |            0.58|               |         0.72|            |not implemented          |
|Ht                           |            0.73|          0.860|         0.70|       0.820|differs                  |
|Longstring                   |            0.58|          0.578|         0.70|       0.685|matches / close          |
|Infit                        |            0.58|               |         0.69|            |not implemented          |
|Outfit                       |            0.59|               |         0.69|            |not implemented          |
|IRV                          |            0.48|          0.482|         0.60|       0.597|matches                  |
|INDCHI                       |            0.56|               |         0.49|            |not implemented          |


**Study 5 - between-subject design (AUC)**



|Index                        | Paper (partial)| cier (partial)| Paper (full)| cier (full)|Verdict (partial / full) |
|:----------------------------|---------------:|--------------:|------------:|-----------:|:------------------------|
|RPR                          |            0.85|          0.845|         0.93|       0.934|close / matches          |
|Antonyms                     |            0.82|               |         0.90|            |abstains                 |
|Antonyms (careful-reference) |            0.82|          0.859|         0.90|       0.933|differs                  |
|Synonyms                     |            0.86|          0.761|         0.90|       0.840|differs                  |
|Synonyms (careful-reference) |            0.86|          0.852|         0.90|       0.911|close                    |
|Time                         |            0.61|               |         0.88|            |not implemented          |
|r_pbis                       |            0.71|          0.709|         0.87|       0.869|matches                  |
|MD                           |            0.81|          0.766|         0.85|       0.746|differs                  |
|lz                           |            0.72|               |         0.83|            |not implemented          |
|Gnormed                      |            0.70|          0.699|         0.80|       0.789|matches / close          |
|Outfit                       |            0.69|               |         0.78|            |not implemented          |
|Infit                        |            0.68|               |         0.77|            |not implemented          |
|Ht                           |            0.65|          0.604|         0.69|       0.641|differs                  |
|Longstring                   |            0.60|          0.599|         0.68|       0.678|matches                  |
|IRV                          |            0.57|          0.567|         0.67|       0.669|matches                  |
|INDCHI                       |            0.48|               |         0.64|            |not implemented          |


**Study 5 - within-subject design (AUC)**



|Index                        | Paper (partial)| cier (partial)| Paper (full)| cier (full)|Verdict (partial / full) |
|:----------------------------|---------------:|--------------:|------------:|-----------:|:------------------------|
|RPR                          |            0.81|          0.805|         0.93|       0.932|close / matches          |
|Time                         |            0.68|               |         0.92|            |not implemented          |
|r_pbis                       |            0.70|          0.695|         0.91|       0.909|matches                  |
|Antonyms                     |            0.74|               |         0.90|            |abstains                 |
|Antonyms (careful-reference) |            0.74|          0.790|         0.90|       0.925|differs / close          |
|Synonyms                     |            0.82|          0.619|         0.90|       0.808|differs                  |
|Synonyms (careful-reference) |            0.82|          0.798|         0.90|       0.916|close                    |
|MD                           |            0.81|          0.607|         0.88|       0.647|differs                  |
|Gnormed                      |            0.72|          0.722|         0.84|       0.831|matches / close          |
|Ht                           |            0.71|          0.846|         0.73|       0.809|differs                  |
|lz                           |            0.50|               |         0.71|            |not implemented          |
|Longstring                   |            0.59|          0.593|         0.69|       0.689|matches                  |
|Outfit                       |            0.50|               |         0.67|            |not implemented          |
|Infit                        |            0.50|               |         0.66|            |not implemented          |
|IRV                          |            0.52|          0.522|         0.66|       0.662|matches                  |
|INDCHI                       |            0.46|               |         0.64|            |not implemented          |

Three patterns in these tables deserve explanation; all three are deliberate
properties of cier's label-free defaults, not statistical disagreements:

- **The pairing indices live or die with the pair search.** Whole-sample
  pairing at the paper's `critical_r = .60` finds no qualifying pairs at all
  on the single-wave Studies 1 and 2 (half the respondents answered
  carelessly, and the inter-item correlations that define the pairs are
  attenuated by exactly that carelessness), so synonyms and antonyms abstain
  there; on the two-wave studies the careless second wave keeps no antonym
  pairs (those cells abstain too) and only a handful of attenuated synonym
  pairs — the default synonym cells are therefore scored but sit well below
  the paper, with each wave pairing on its own correlations. The paper could
  instead pair on its known-careful respondents; an applied screen has no
  such group. The *(careful-reference)* companion rows reproduce that
  label-using construction for Studies 4 and 5 — at the same `.60` threshold
  the pair search on the careful conditions' second wave succeeds, and the
  resulting AUCs track the paper closely. On Studies 1 and 2 even the careful
  subsample yields no pairs at `.60` (the single-administration correlations
  are weaker), so no companion rows are shown; the authors' own stored
  synonym/antonym scores confirm the index works once pairs exist.
- **Mahalanobis distance is the one real divergence.** cier centres and
  scales on the whole sample; the paper used the careful respondents as the
  reference. With half the sample careless, the whole-sample centroid is
  pulled toward the careless responses and they stop looking like outliers —
  most visibly in the within-subject contrasts of Studies 4 and 5. cier keeps
  the whole-sample default because a Mahalanobis reference is only
  trustworthy when the reference group is genuinely clean, and in applied
  screening no such group is known.
- **Ht's reference set shifts its two-wave contrasts both ways.** The
  per-domain scalability here is computed on the full wave rather than the
  paper's careful-referenced construction: the within-subject contrasts
  separate *more* strongly than the paper reports (positive deltas in the WS
  tables), while two between-subject cells on Study 5 sit slightly below the
  paper. The statistic itself matches `mokken` — the divergence is the
  reference sample, in both directions.

The remaining battery indices — RPR, the person-total correlation, longstring,
IRV, and Gnormed — reproduce the paper across all twelve study-design-level
cells at or near the paper's own precision. For RPR this requires the split
halves to be drawn *within the 15 BFI-2 facets*: domain-level halves wash out
the partially careless signal and lose four to six AUC points on the partial
cells.

### Goldammer et al. (2024), RPR paper


**Real-data example, 6 facets**



|Index  |Statistic | Paper|  cier|  Delta|Verdict       |
|:------|:---------|-----:|-----:|------:|:-------------|
|PR     |AUC       | 0.650| 0.651|  0.001|matches       |
|RPR25  |AUC       | 0.687| 0.686| -0.001|matches       |
|RPR50  |AUC       | 0.693| 0.691| -0.002|matches       |
|RPR100 |AUC       | 0.684| 0.687|  0.003|close         |
|PR     |SEN95     |      |      |       |not evaluated |
|RPR25  |SEN95     | 0.092| 0.126|  0.034|differs       |
|RPR50  |SEN95     | 0.113| 0.118|  0.005|close         |
|RPR100 |SEN95     | 0.105| 0.143|  0.038|differs       |


**Real-data example, 16 facets**



|Index  |Statistic | Paper|  cier|  Delta|Verdict |
|:------|:---------|-----:|-----:|------:|:-------|
|PR     |AUC       | 0.933| 0.934|  0.001|matches |
|RPR25  |AUC       | 0.955| 0.958|  0.003|matches |
|RPR50  |AUC       | 0.956| 0.957|  0.001|matches |
|RPR100 |AUC       | 0.959| 0.957| -0.002|matches |
|PR     |SEN95     | 0.744| 0.782|  0.038|differs |
|RPR25  |SEN95     | 0.815| 0.782| -0.033|differs |
|RPR50  |SEN95     | 0.786| 0.786|  0.000|matches |
|RPR100 |SEN95     | 0.828| 0.803| -0.025|close   |


**Real-data example, 23 facets**



|Index  |Statistic | Paper|  cier|  Delta|Verdict |
|:------|:---------|-----:|-----:|------:|:-------|
|PR     |AUC       | 0.942| 0.943|  0.001|matches |
|RPR25  |AUC       | 0.963| 0.964|  0.001|matches |
|RPR50  |AUC       | 0.965| 0.965|  0.000|matches |
|RPR100 |AUC       | 0.964| 0.964|  0.000|matches |
|PR     |SEN95     | 0.749| 0.744| -0.005|close   |
|RPR25  |SEN95     | 0.845| 0.861|  0.016|close   |
|RPR50  |SEN95     | 0.887| 0.870| -0.017|close   |
|RPR100 |SEN95     | 0.870| 0.861| -0.009|close   |

The AUCs reproduce the paper's Table 9 to three decimals (conventional PR is
the even-odd consistency; cier's value correlates -1.0000 with the authors'
stored PR column). The SEN95 rows are the one place the estimator itself
differs: the paper smooths the ROC curve with a tie-corrected nonparametric
regression, while the check here uses the plain empirical 95%-specificity
cutoff, which is why several SEN95 cells sit a few points off while their AUC
rows match. The paper's footnote that PR cannot reach a 5% false-positive
rate with six facets reproduces too: the empirical cutoff yields zero
sensitivity for that cell.

### Schroeders et al. (2022)


**Empirical study, mean over 1,000 test draws (10% prevalence)**



|Index                   |Statistic         | Paper|  cier|  Delta|Verdict         |
|:-----------------------|:-----------------|-----:|-----:|------:|:---------------|
|Mahalanobis             |accuracy          |  0.80| 0.806|  0.006|close           |
|Mahalanobis             |sensitivity       |  0.18| 0.178| -0.002|matches         |
|Mahalanobis             |specificity       |  0.87| 0.876|  0.006|close           |
|Mahalanobis             |precision         |  0.13| 0.137|  0.007|close           |
|Mahalanobis             |balanced accuracy |  0.53| 0.527| -0.003|matches         |
|Antonyms                |accuracy          |  0.86| 0.857| -0.003|matches         |
|Antonyms                |sensitivity       |  0.11| 0.111|  0.001|matches         |
|Antonyms                |specificity       |  0.94| 0.940|  0.000|matches         |
|Antonyms                |precision         |  0.17| 0.172|  0.002|matches         |
|Antonyms                |balanced accuracy |  0.53| 0.525| -0.005|close           |
|Even-odd                |accuracy          |  0.84| 0.842|  0.002|matches         |
|Even-odd                |sensitivity       |  0.19| 0.188| -0.002|matches         |
|Even-odd                |specificity       |  0.91| 0.914|  0.004|matches         |
|Even-odd                |precision         |  0.19| 0.194|  0.004|matches         |
|Even-odd                |balanced accuracy |  0.55| 0.551|  0.001|matches         |
|Longstring              |accuracy          |  0.82| 0.822|  0.002|matches         |
|Longstring              |sensitivity       |  0.18| 0.178| -0.002|matches         |
|Longstring              |specificity       |  0.90| 0.894| -0.006|close           |
|Longstring              |precision         |  0.16| 0.155| -0.005|matches         |
|Longstring              |balanced accuracy |  0.54| 0.536| -0.004|matches         |
|IRV                     |accuracy          |  0.83| 0.827| -0.003|matches         |
|IRV                     |sensitivity       |  0.14| 0.137| -0.003|matches         |
|IRV                     |specificity       |  0.90| 0.904|  0.004|matches         |
|IRV                     |precision         |  0.14| 0.136| -0.004|matches         |
|IRV                     |balanced accuracy |  0.52| 0.520|  0.000|matches         |
|Zh                      |accuracy          |  0.67|      |       |not implemented |
|Zh                      |sensitivity       |  0.38|      |       |not implemented |
|Zh                      |specificity       |  0.70|      |       |not implemented |
|Zh                      |precision         |  0.12|      |       |not implemented |
|Zh                      |balanced accuracy |  0.54|      |       |not implemented |
|GBM (responses)         |accuracy          |  0.59|      |       |not implemented |
|GBM (responses)         |sensitivity       |  0.61|      |       |not implemented |
|GBM (responses)         |specificity       |  0.58|      |       |not implemented |
|GBM (responses)         |precision         |  0.14|      |       |not implemented |
|GBM (responses)         |balanced accuracy |  0.60|      |       |not implemented |
|GBM (response times)    |accuracy          |  0.70|      |       |not implemented |
|GBM (response times)    |sensitivity       |  0.56|      |       |not implemented |
|GBM (response times)    |specificity       |  0.71|      |       |not implemented |
|GBM (response times)    |precision         |  0.18|      |       |not implemented |
|GBM (response times)    |balanced accuracy |  0.64|      |       |not implemented |
|GBM (responses + times) |accuracy          |  0.70|      |       |not implemented |
|GBM (responses + times) |sensitivity       |  0.60|      |       |not implemented |
|GBM (responses + times) |specificity       |  0.71|      |       |not implemented |
|GBM (responses + times) |precision         |  0.19|      |       |not implemented |
|GBM (responses + times) |balanced accuracy |  0.66|      |       |not implemented |

The five traditional indices cier implements reproduce the paper's means at
its two-decimal precision (the redrawn test samples differ from the authors',
but a mean over 1,000 draws pins these metrics down to ~0.002). The paper's
substantive point survives the reproduction intact: at a realistic 10%
prevalence every traditional index keeps high specificity but low sensitivity,
and the boosting models trade some specificity for sensitivity without
reaching comfortable precision either.

## Reproducing this table

The assembled comparison ships with the package:


``` r
tab <- read.csv(system.file("extdata", "published-results.csv",
                            package = "cier"))
subset(tab, status == "differs",
       c(paper, study, design, level, index, paper_value, cier_value, delta))
```

Re-running the computations requires network access (the Goldammer archives
and the Schroeders data are fetched into `tools::R_user_dir("cier", "cache")`;
they carry no redistribution licence, so cier never bundles them), the
suggested packages `PerFit` and `mokken`, and the seeds recorded at the top of
this document. The package's slow-tier test suite performs exactly that
re-run and holds every cier value in the committed table to 5e-4.

## References

- Brühlmann, F., Petralito, S., Aeschbach, L. F., & Opwis, K. (2020). The
  quality of data collected online: An investigation of careless responding
  in a crowdsourced sample. *Methods in Psychology*, 2, 100022.
- Goldammer, P., Stöckli, P. L., Escher, Y. A., Annen, H., Jonas, K., &
  Antonakis, J. (2024). Careless responding detection revisited: Accuracy of
  direct and indirect measures. *Behavior Research Methods*, 56(8),
  8422-8449.
- Goldammer, P., Stöckli, P. L., Annen, H., & Schmitz-Wilhelmy, A. (2024). A
  comparison of conventional and resampled personal reliability in detecting
  careless responding. *Behavior Research Methods*, 56(8), 8831-8851.
- Schroeders, U., Schmidt, C., & Gnambs, T. (2022). Detecting careless
  responding in survey data using stochastic gradient boosting. *Educational
  and Psychological Measurement*, 82(1), 29-56.
